rulings.Case Title: SUSY CHEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Amend First Amended Petition For Writ
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER SUSY CHEN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT is OFF CALENDAR. Heard on January 23, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Answer To Plaintiffs' Verified Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO PLAINTIFFS' VERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. August 9, 2022 answer is stricken. An amended answer filed by only one answering party superseded the answer on file and leave the record uncertain. rulings.Case Title: HELEN OKAMOTO ET AL VS. JANE BING LEE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT JANE LEE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and denied in part. While the complaint is dismissed, the Court will rule on the merits of the motion for the purposes of determination of the prevailing party. The motion is granted as to the following parts of the Complaint: a. The words "filing a frivolous lawsuit. CGC-597416" in paragraph 4, 3:2-5; b. Sentence "Finally, in January 2022, Mr. Wasserman resorted to using a frivolous lawsuit to harass and intimidated Plaintiffs. CGC-22-597416." 8, 3:25-27; c. Portions of paragraph 11: "After filing his lawsuit, in depositions, Mr. Wasserman badgered Plaintiff Helen Okamoto with irrelevant questions intended only to harass her" at 4:21-23; "due to the lawsuit" at 4:23; and "and lawsuit" at 4:26. d. The words "with a frivolus lawsuit" in pragaph 17 at 5:29. The motion is otherwise denied. The Court finds that Defendant prevailed on this motion and is entitled to recover reasonable attorney's fees. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HELEN OKAMOTO ET AL VS. JANE BING LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Of Defendant Lee S Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 (Anti-Slapp) Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Plaintiffs Okamoto And Owyang
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2023 line 7. MOTION OF DEFENDANT LEE'S CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 (ANTI-SLAPP) MOTION TO STRIKE THE COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFFS OKAMOTO AND OWYANG is GRANTED in part as follows: 1. The words: "and by filing a frivolous lawsuit. CGC-22-594417" in paragraph 4. 2. The word: "to the lawsuit" and "and lawsuit" in paragraph 10. The motion is otherwise denied. Notice of motion does not contain the specific language Defendants seek to strike (CRC 3.1322) and the Court could not locate the language quoted in the Point and Authorities at 3:15-22) in the operative complaint filed in action CGC-22-603547 on December 20, 2022. Compare, for example, quote purportedly located at paragraph 16, page 5:7-9 "Mr. Wasserman  knew that the only court of action for Plaintiffs to collect the relocation fee due them was to file a lawsuit" and paragraph 16 of the Complaint on file. Also see paragraph 11 quoted in the Points and Authorities and paragraph 11 of the Complaint. The Court, having reviewed a similar motion in case CGC-22-603454 assumes that this motion was recycled without sufficient editing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
